records.ety: [L. sensualis, from sensus sense: cf. F. sensuel.]

defs: Pertaining to, consisting in, or affecting, the sense, or bodily organs of perception; relating to, or concerning, the body, in distinction from the spirit.
quotes: Pleasing and sensual rites and ceremonies.
Far as creation's ample range extends, The scale of sensual, mental powers ascends.

defs: Hence, not spiritual or intellectual; carnal; fleshly; pertaining to, or consisting in, the gratification of the senses, or the indulgence of appetites; wordly.
quotes: These be they who separate themselves, sensual, having not the Spirit.
The greatest part of men are such as prefer . . . that good which is sensual before whatsoever is most divine.

defs: Devoted to the pleasures of sense and appetite; luxurious; voluptuous; lewd; libidinous.
quotes: No small part of virtue consists in abstaining from that wherein sensual men place their felicity.

defs: Pertaining or peculiar to the philosophical doctrine of sensualism.
